title: Write a CloudFormation Template
description:
## Infrastructure as Code on AWS

CloudFormation provisions AWS resources declaratively from a template.

**Your task:** write a CloudFormation template at **`/root/template.yaml`** with a **`Resources`** section that defines an **`AWS::S3::Bucket`** resource.

```yaml
Resources:
  ReportsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: my-reports-bucket
```

> 💡 Keep it plain YAML (no `!Ref`/`!GetAtt` intrinsics needed here) so it parses cleanly.

hints.title: Minimal bucket template
hints.body: Under Resources, a logical name maps to a Type of AWS::S3::Bucket with optional Properties.
hints.command:
printf 'Resources:
  ReportsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: my-reports-bucket
' > /root/template.yaml

validation.description: Checks for a Resources section containing an AWS::S3::Bucket.

commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('/root/template.yaml')); r=d.get('Resources',{}); print('ok' if isinstance(r,dict) and any(isinstance(v,dict) and v.get('Type')=='AWS::S3::Bucket' for v in r.values()) else 'no')"
